description: Lockfile for the vendored frontend UMD libraries under app/web/vendor/. Each library lives in its own subfolder (xterm/, chartjs/, ajv/). Every file in those subfolders MUST have a corresponding entry in `files` below, keyed by relative path from app/web/vendor/. At broker startup, each file's SHA-256 is recomputed and compared against `sha256`; mismatch aborts startup with exit code 5. UMD libraries are not Authenticode-signed; SHA-256 + canonical first-party download URL are the integrity attestations. No runtime download / fetch / CDN reference is ever used.

procurement.method: Direct HTTP GET of canonical npm registry .tgz files from registry.npmjs.org. No package manager used. Each tarball was extracted via in-box tar.exe; only the listed UMD asset was copied; the remainder of each tarball was discarded.
procurement.notes: Deterministic one-time vendoring. To re-vendor: download each .tgz from sourceUrl, extract with tar.exe, copy the file at extractedFrom, then regenerate this lockfile.

chartjs/chart.umd.js.note: GitHub releases for chart.js v4.4.4 do not ship a pre-built UMD asset tarball at the expected /releases/download/v4.4.4/ path; the canonical npm tarball was used instead. registry.npmjs.org is the official npm distribution and chart.js's GitHub release page links to it.

ajv/ajv.bundle.js.note: Ajv 8.x dropped pre-built UMD bundles from its npm tarball (CommonJS only). Ajv 6.12.6 is the last version with a self-contained UMD bundle (dist/ajv.bundle.js, all deps inlined). Ajv 6 supports JSON Schema draft-07 which is sufficient for the recipe schema.
